title: Fundamental Concepts of Group Theory
description: A visual guide showing the core components and properties that define a group in abstract algebra.
relationships: A Set G is combined with a Binary Operation (*) to form a Group (G, *)
The operation must satisfy Closure: ∀ a,b ∈ G, a*b ∈ G
The operation must be Associative: ∀ a,b,c ∈ G, (a*b)*c = a*(b*c)
The Set must contain an Identity Element e: ∀ a ∈ G, a*e = e*a = a
Every Element must have an Inverse: ∀ a ∈ G, ∃ a⁻¹ ∈ G such that a*a⁻¹ = a⁻¹*a = e
